news.title:
the islamic regime in iran has been murdering dissidents and freedom fighters for decades.

in recen
news.summary:
the islamic regime in iran has been murdering dissidents and freedom fighters for decades.

in recent weeks, they have been only speeding up their so-called “trials” and executions.

they must be stopped once and for all…


gazelle غزاله شارمهد (@gazellesharmahd)

🔥we have the names of every official currently negotiating a deal with my father’s murderers. this is for them:

policymakers. negotiators. geopolitical “experts” weighing the costs and benefits of a deal with the islamic regime.

let me show you somETHing.

this is my father’s funeral. jamshid sharmahd. taken from us, tortured, murdered — by the regime you are sitting across from right now.

i was taught never to cry in front of the enemy. 47 years taught all of us that. these monsters collect our grief like a trophy. showing pain gives them pleasure.

i am 속보 that rule today.

because you need to see what “a deal” actually costs. not in 제재 relief numbers. not in geopolitical leverage. 

in this. 

in a family standing over a casket they fought years just to bring home.

we are not a file on your desk. 
we are not a variable in negotiation.
we are americans. 
we are germans. 
we are iranians.
we are what happens when savages are rewarded with survival.

90 million iranians are silenced in a blackout right now. we are their voice. and every iranian patriot in the diaspora has paid — a father, a limb, an eye, a life — to be that voice.

you do not get to have an opinion on this deal until you have carried your own flesh and blood home in a casket.

i have.
we all have.

look at this video. don’t look away.

then tell me — which side do you stand on?

video
  





— https://nitter.net/gazellesharmahd/status/2046289824496427272#m

news.title:
iran will be free.

millions of iranians rose up across the country in january.

this isn’t over…
news.summary:
iran will be free.

millions of iranians rose up across the country in january.

this isn’t over…


decado (@itsdecado)

one hundred days have passed since those two nights. 

the two nights where i felt the meaning of everything everywhere all at once. i was full of joy, pride, anger, worry, despair, uncertainty, hope, belief, and everything in between. they were as glorious as they were sorrowful. a nation was telling a romantic, draMATIC, violent love story. the story of iran and our abSOLute love for it.

what transpired after is not that different. those of us who are survivors of the january massacre feel like we are still there. we are still experiencing the exact same wave of emotions. 

we remember. 

we remember the heavy metallic smell of gunpowder in the air. we remember the ash of the fires we lit up dancing in the cold wind. we remember how we illuminated a sky that stood darker than ever, because the occupying syndicate had initiated a total blackout. digital and electrical. they tried to 급락 us into the abSOLute dark. 

we remember how the light of the phones they had severed from the outside world became beacons in our hands. we held them up in the dark, desperate to show the world exactly how many of us were fighting and how we were holding the line. we remember how we felt when we stood around the fires we started, chanting javid shah togETHer. it felt like that fire was our collective will. we stood around it because our unity was the very thing breathing the fire of courage into every single one of us that night.

the words i had held dear for so long hit me deep in my core when i was standing beside that fire, taking my place in the lion and sun revolution. i remember them echoing throughout my entire existence:

"the legends of my homeland are not forgotten myths,
my people still breathe fire through the darkest rifts.
alive and standing firm where shadows meet,
fear and ignorance have died upon their feet."

i remember being amazed to see our true flags everywhere, because i knew exactly how hard it was to acquire one under the gaze of a terrorist syndicate.
i remember the rapid gunshots.
i remember the taste of tear gas in my throat, the ache of my mussels throughout my bosand the sickening worry over my friends and loved ones burning a hole inside my gut. and i remember seeing the spark of pure, undeniable fear in the eyes of their thugs. 

i remember all of it.
my rage will not let me forget a single second. 

each moment of each day, awake or asleep, i feel that exact same mixture of feelings while i watch foreign politicians play their diploMATIC games and broadcast their confusing noise. and that is exactly how i know in my heart that we will end this regime one way or another. because even if the world turns away, i know i have my compatriots to rely on, both outside and inside this country. i know we will keep moving forward no matter what.

now, to those sitting in abSOLute safety who tell us to just go pick up a gun and fight for our freedom:

speaking as an iranian civilian locked inside this country, first i have to ask you, where the hell do i get a gun or the ammunition to load it? i am not saying we cannot get weapons. i am trying to tell you that what you say is infinitely harder than it sounds from your safe distance. 

but regardless of your advice, be abSOLutely sure of this fact:

we are not sitting by asking for our freedom to be handed to us on a silver platter like some privileged kid.

we have already fought and bled on the asphalt for our liberation. when the time comes, we will do so again. 

we will not forget. we will not forgive. and we will take our country back.

pāyandeh #irán.
javid shah.

video
  





— https://nitter.net/itsdecado/status/2045594388747714959#m

according to embercn, after 30,766 ETH
news.summary:
아비트럼 freeze of kelpdao-linked ETH triggers faster laundering

according to embercn, after 30,766 ETH tied to the kelpdao exploit was frozen on 아비트럼, the attacker started moving around 75,700 ETH on 이더리움 mainnet, worth about $175 million. 

multiple small ETH transfers have already gone through umbracash, suggesting accelerated laundering and fund-splitting.
nitter.net/embercn/status/2046508…

news.title:
curve founder calls for industry-wide 디파이 security standards

curve founder michael egorov calls for
news.summary:
curve founder calls for industry-wide 디파이 security standards

curve founder michael egorov calls for industry-wide 디파이 security standards to address recent preventable hacks caused by centralized single points of failure. 

citing the aave and rsETH incidents, he urged projects, auditors, and risk teams to share best practices, suggesting the 이더리움 and 솔라나 foundations should take the lead in establishing these safety guidelines.
nitter.net/newmichwill/status/204…

news.title:
dune analyzes layerzero oapp security: 47% rely on minimum 1-of-1 dvn setup

according to dune, foll
news.summary:
dune analyzes layerzero oapp security: 47% rely on minimum 1-of-1 dvn setup

according to dune, following the kelpdao hack, it analyzed the dvn security configurations of active oapps on layerzero over the past 90 days. 

the data reveals that among approximately 2,665 unique oapp contracts, 47% run on the 1-of-1 dvn security floor, 45% use 2-of-2, and only about 5% use 3-of-3 or higher configurations. notably, kelpdao's rsETH falls into the lowest 1-of-1 security category.
nitter.net/dune/status/2046257791…

news.title: kelpdao thanked the 아비트럼 security council for its emergency freeze action, saying the team has worke
news.summary: kelpdao thanked the 아비트럼 security council for its emergency freeze action, saying the team has worked closely with the council and ecosystem stakeholders over the past two days. it also credited seal 911 for helping coordinate the response. kelpdao said its focus remains supporting rsETH holders and minimizing the broader impact on 디파이.

news.title:
속보:

japanese pm sanae takaichi decides to ease the country’s restrictive rules on arms exports.

th
news.summary:
속보:

japanese pm sanae takaichi decides to ease the country’s restrictive rules on arms exports.

the government has announced that the previous ban on exports had to be relaxed in order to stimulate the domestic defense industry.

the export of some defensive equipment, such as mine sweepers and rADArs, has been allowed since shinzo abe changed the law in 2014 but the new guidelines will make it possible for allied countries to buy offensive weapons such fighter jets, cruise missiles and warships.

japan’s decision is great news for its allies in north america and europe which have depleted weapon stocks after the wars in ukraine and iran, with their industrial capacity struggling to keep up with the demand for new short-term arms production

🇺🇸🇨🇦🇪🇺🇺🇦🇯🇵

news.title:
마켓이 계속 상승하는 배경에는...

수급적인 측면도 있지만,

매크로 면에서 환경이 개선되는 기대감이 있습니다. 

fci 완화, 유가는 호르무즈 개방 기대와 수요가 축소되며 하
news.summary:
마켓이 계속 상승하는 배경에는...

수급적인 측면도 있지만,

매크로 면에서 환경이 개선되는 기대감이 있습니다. 

fci 완화, 유가는 호르무즈 개방 기대와 수요가 축소되며 하락, ppi 예상치 하회. 

지금 시기가 지나고나면... 소비자 구매력이 살아날 여지가 있어 보이죠. 

마치 팬데믹 시절처럼.

호르무즈가 열리면 원자재 비용 압력도 추가로 빠질 가능성이 높습니다.

여기에 하나 더.. 11월은 중간선거입니다.

트럼프 입장에서 중간선거 전까지 주가 관리는 필수죠.

지금처럼 트럼프 지지율이 바닥인 상태에서... 

공화당 의석을 지키려면 경제가 좋아 보여야 하고, 

미국에서 경제를 설명하는 1번 지표는 S&P 500이니까요. 

실제로 트럼프는 이미 taco발 휴전 중재, 원유 가격 하락 같은 긍정적 발언으로 시장을 여러 차례 띄웠습니다. 

시장은 이걸 알고 있고.. 

그래서 "트럼프가 시장을 망가뜨리지 않을 것"이라는 암묵적 풋옵션처럼 작동하고 있습니다. 

앞으로 6개월, 정치적 유인이 유동성 기대와 맞물려 있는 구간입니다.

그동안 분석했듯이... 구조적 리스크도 분명히 있습니다. 

그리고.. 월말 리밸런싱 수요는 채권 쪽으로 쏠릴 가능성이 있습니다.

골드만 john flood의 표현이 지금 시장 분위기를 가장 잘 요약합니다. 

"이 정도면 좀 과한 느낌이다. 그로스 레버리지는 여전히 너무 높고, 넷 레버리지는 너무 낮다. 풀백의 완벽한 조건이 갖춰져 있다."

ai가 실제로 기업 이익을 확장시키고 있다는 증거가 계속 나온다면, 이제부터 진짜 새로운 사이클의 시작일 수 있습니다. 

반대로 이익 가시성 없이 포지셔닝 수급만으로 올라온 거라면, 되돌림은 생각보다 빠를 수 있습니다.

이제 답은 지금 쏟아지는 빅테크 실적에 있다는 말이겠죠.

news.title: 🇺🇦 gunfire reported in odessa after an apparent clash between sbu security service officers and mili
news.summary:
🇺🇦 gunfire reported in odessa after an apparent clash between sbu security service officers and military conscription enforcers.

- footage shows a man desperately trying to break free from a van during a detention
- sbu officers and military recruitment officials reportedly involved
- a second vehicle that fled the scene has also been apprehended

the sbu is ukraine's domestic intelligence agency, roughly equivalent to the fbi.

ukraine's mobilization law allows for eligible men to be detained on the spot anywhere they are found... men have jumped from windows, bribed officials, and fled the country to avoid deployment.

source: rt and tg channels

news.title:
🇮🇷 50 days of war, $50 billion in oil lost, 500 million barrels that never made it to market.

that'
news.summary:
🇮🇷 50 days of war, $50 billion in oil lost, 500 million barrels that never made it to market.

that's enough to fuel the entire world's shipping industry for 4 months, or cover nearly a month of u.s. oil demand.

gas is sitting at $4.04 a gallon on average in the u.s. right now, up 37% since the war began. 

energy experts say even a ceasefire won't bring prices back for months.

the iea is calling this "the largest energy crisis we have ever faced."

source: ap news

news.title: 🇪🇺 that summer vacation you’ve been planning may not be happening… at least not for the price you ex
news.summary:
🇪🇺 that summer vacation you’ve been planning may not be happening… at least not for the price you expected.

the latest war in the middle east has sent fuel prices surging, and airlines are having to make decisions about which routes survive and which get cut.

in the next few weeks, airports could start running dry, with london among the most vulnerable.

european carriers are expected to start rationing fuel and prioritizing long-haul transatlantic flights over short-haul routes within the continent.

the world cup kicks off in north america in 7 weeks, with millions of fans planning to fly in from europe and asia.

but the fuel to get them there is either going up or running out.

the iran war has wreaked all kinds of havoc, and that vacation you’ve been planning is just more collateral damage.

news.summary:

- accuses the u.s. of being "overly demanding" and using talks for domestic political gain
- cites u.s. "recent aggressions" and iran's history with washington as reasons to walk away
- "given current conditions, the next round of talks is, god willing, off the table"

news.title:
massive buybacks are coming to the us market:

buyback authorizations across the russell 3000 급등d +3
news.summary:
massive buybacks are coming to the us market:

buyback authorizations across the russell 3000 급등d +36% yoy, to a record $428 billion so far in 2026.

authorizations are now +176% above the same period in 2020.

at a historical execution rate of ~90%, us corporates are now on track to repurchase a record ~$1 trillion in shares this year.

in 2 weeks, the corporate buyback window will reopen following the earnings blackout period.

this will bring an additional wave of demand for stocks at a time when the market is already experiencing a historic run.

us corporations are set to be major buyers in the equity market.

news.title:
운명의 화요일이다.  

여러 잡음에도 불구하고, 협상은 잘될 거라고 보는 분위기더라.   

당연히 잘돼야지, 시발..

🇫🇷🇦🇫 french authorities have arrested a 19-ye
news.summary:
no editorializing needed.


visegrád 24 (@visegrad24)

🇫🇷🇦🇫 french authorities have arrested a 19-year-old afghan migrant accused of a series of brutal sexual assaults on goats and sheep at a farm near marseille, france.

the investigation began after the owners of the farm discovered several of their animals in severe distress with their legs tied and bleeding from genital injuries. one of the victims is a five-month-old lamb that nearly lost its life from the afghan teen's abuse.

surveillance cameras captured the young migrant performing violent sexual acts with the animals.

he was caught in the act and taken into custody. while the suspect has reportedly denied the accusations, prosecutors stated that dna evidence from semen samples found on six of the animals matches him.





— https://nitter.net/visegrad24/status/2046367148478693646#m
